name: Gijón, El Arroyo La Ñora, el Faro de Torres – circular por la costa
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Gijón/Xixón offer a diverse landscape for riders, blending urban energy with extensive natural surroundings. The region features a varied terrain of meadows, hills, and coastal paths, with over 80% of its municipality being rural. Riders can explore routes that traverse both the coastline and inland areas, encountering significant elevation changes and natural features like the Arroyo La Ñora and Monte Deva. This varied topography provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Gijón/Xixón The most popular mountain bike trail is Gijón, El Arroyo La Ñora, el Faro de Torres – circular por la costa, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers coastal views and passes by the Faro de Torres. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Gijón to Tazones Loop, a difficult 40.9 miles (65.9 km) path. This challenging route includes significant elevation gain and explores varied terrain between Gijón and Tazones. Local mountain bikers also love the Gijón, Arroyo La Ñora, Monte Deva, Necrópolis Cerro de los Llanos y Túmulos de San Pablo – circular, a 37.4 miles (60.1 km) trail leading through Monte Deva and the Arroyo La Ñora, often completed in about 4 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Gijón/Xixón is defined by coastal paths, rural landscapes, and areas like Monte Deva and the Vía Verde de La Camocha. The network offers options for different ability levels, from family-friendly greenways to more technical trails. The routes in Gijón/Xixón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gijón/Xixón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking in Gijón/Xixón?
A: The trails around Gijón/Xixón offer a good mix of challenges. You'll find approximately 18 easy routes, 57 moderate routes, and 34 difficult routes, ensuring options for all experience levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón benefits from a mild climate, making it enjoyable for mountain biking throughout the year. The absence of suffocating heat, even in summer, means you can comfortably explore the trails in most seasons.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Yes, Gijón/Xixón has family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40357561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Camocha loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, following an old mining railway through forests and over bridges, offering a pleasant and accessible experience for families.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Gijón/Xixón combine rural landscapes with stunning coastal paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027483678/gijon-el-arroyo-la-nora-el-faro-de-torres-circular-por-la-costa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gijón, El Arroyo La Ñora, el Faro de Torres – circular por la costa&lt;/a&gt; route provides excellent coastal views and passes by the Faro de Torres.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with significant elevation changes for a challenge?
A: Yes, Gijón/Xixón's diverse terrain includes routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027487214/gijon-to-tazones-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gijón to Tazones Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 40.9-mile path with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, offering a challenging ride for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along mountain bike trails in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Mountain bike trails in Gijón/Xixón often pass by various natural features and landmarks. You might encounter areas like Arroyo La Ñora and Monte Deva, or viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cervigón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Providencia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering spectacular views of the Cantabrian Sea and the city.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, Gijón/Xixón offers several circular mountain bike routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027488290/gijon-arroyo-la-nora-monte-deva-necropolis-cerro-de-los-llanos-y-tumulos-de-san-pablo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gijón, Arroyo La Ñora, Monte Deva, Necrópolis Cerro de los Llanos y Túmulos de San Pablo – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through Monte Deva and Arroyo La Ñora.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Gijón/Xixón?
A: The mountain biking experience in Gijón/Xixón is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, blending coastal paths with rural landscapes, and the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that go through Monte Deva?
A: Yes, Monte Deva is a popular area for mountain biking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Deva Trail – Monte Deva loop from Estación de Gijón/Xixón Sanz Crespo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.7 km route that explores this scenic area, known for its recreational spots and views.

Q: Are there options for mountain bikers looking for technical challenges?
A: Gijón/Xixón provides technical elements for experienced riders. Areas like Cerro Gavío or Cimero offer trails with significant elevation changes and technical features. The 'Los Vivales' circuit is even used for enduro competitions, indicating the presence of advanced and thrilling trails.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails using public transport in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón has a commitment to active tourism and sustainable transportation. While specific trailheads may vary, the city offers a public electric bike-sharing service, 'Gijón Bici,' and a network of bike lanes and cycle paths, which can help in accessing various starting points for your mountain biking adventures.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, some routes may pass by historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027488290/gijon-arroyo-la-nora-monte-deva-necropolis-cerro-de-los-llanos-y-tumulos-de-san-pablo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gijón, Arroyo La Ñora, Monte Deva, Necrópolis Cerro de los Llanos y Túmulos de San Pablo – circular&lt;/a&gt; route includes the Necrópolis Cerro de los Llanos y Túmulos de San Pablo, offering a glimpse into the region's past.
tips: Attention, there are also hikers on the way. The descent is technically demanding.
